Those who genuinely love God can expect specific blessings: God will manifest Himself to them, speak to them personally, empower them to obey His commandments, and prepare extraordinary things for their lives that eye has not seen.

1OnoVf7exSRx2UagGPDXmkWCSEmgQTSCH.title: 05.14 - 7 Lessons Learnt From The Life of Our Founder
1OnoVf7exSRx2UagGPDXmkWCSEmgQTSCH.summary: BJosh teaches seven lessons from Bishop Dag Heward-Mills' life: the grace of God that takes someone from small beginnings to great impact, obedience to God's call even when difficult, faithfulness over decades as God's requirement, patience after doing God's will, sacrifice and losing things to follow Christ, proving love for God through action not just words, and the sustaining presence of God through every trial. He emphasizes that the same grace that built the ministry from a classroom to thousands of churches is available to all believers who will obey, remain faithful, and walk in God's will. He challenges listeners to be partakers of this grace and declares that though their beginning may be small, their latter end will greatly increase if they follow these principles.

1mXTmV9-M1Puj1us_Y91irIfVTyMTkMu1.title: 2023.01.15FLCES_Reasons Why Everybody Must Pray
1mXTmV9-M1Puj1us_Y91irIfVTyMTkMu1.summary: BJosh teaches that Christians must stop worrying about life's needs (food, clothing, money, relationships) and instead focus on seeking God's kingdom first through prayer and fasting, trusting that God will add all these things supernaturally. He announces a three-week prayer operation called "Don't Think About It" with daily prayer meetings and fasting, culminating in a week of withdrawal to seek God's face. He emphasizes that prayer is a great act of faith that builds spiritual awareness and transforms lives, especially through speaking in tongues which makes believers more conscious of the spirit world and God's presence.

1h2LE-jz1H0n4P9HQ8fiIIz03V6rrcOnJ.title: 2017.06.11FLTC_The Art Of Following David(The Art Of FOllowing)
1h2LE-jz1H0n4P9HQ8fiIIz03V6rrcOnJ.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that Christians often don't know what or how to pray, but the Holy Spirit helps this infirmity by interceding through speaking in tongues, which allows believers to pray mysteries according to God's will rather than their own desires. He illustrates the art of following by showing how Jacob copied Esau to obtain blessings he had no birthright to, arguing that believers must similarly emulate spiritual fathers like David to receive blessings beyond their natural origins. The core message is that praying in tongues connects believers directly to God's mind through the Spirit, bypassing human limitations and fleshly desires.
1h2LE-jz1H0n4P9HQ8fiIIz03V6rrcOnJ.keyScripture_elaboration: Romans 8:26-27 is the theological foundation of the entire sermon, with Bishop Joshua spending significant time expounding how the Spirit helps our weakness of not knowing what to pray for by making intercession through groanings and tongues.

18j8J7ERI4fdHAkv7wmkEKBNc9Vstnh5v.title: 2017.04.16FLTC_The Fellowship of His Suffering (Losing, Suffering, Sacrificing, Dying)_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
18j8J7ERI4fdHAkv7wmkEKBNc9Vstnh5v.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that God wants believers to experience suffering because it brings them closer to Christ, just as ultimate closeness comes through death being absent from the body and present with the Lord. He explains that there are progressive levels of sacrifice—from losing things once gained, to counting all things as loss and dung, to forgetting past things entirely—and that believers can only be as close to Christ as their sacrifice is close to His sacrifice. He emphasizes that those who wait on the Lord and suffer with Christ exist at a higher spiritual level, like eagles flying above ground-level traps, and that many Christians cannot relate to truly spiritual people because they haven't paid the same price through prayer, fasting, and denying worldly pleasures.

18LLbjnItXkkxZ5cvqbwSRXB3HBrdpGi8.title: 2017.04.02FLTC_Reasons Why God Wants You to Experience Suffering (Losing, Suffering, Sacrificing, Dying)_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
18LLbjnItXkkxZ5cvqbwSRXB3HBrdpGi8.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that suffering, losing, sacrificing, and dying to self must be reintroduced into Christian theology because believers are called not only to believe but also to suffer. He emphasizes that Moses chose to suffer affliction with God's people rather than enjoy the pleasures of sin, and that it actually pleases God when His people suffer and take up their cross. He challenges the congregation to identify specific areas where they need to lose something, suffer inconvenience, or sacrifice comfort, arguing that without visible suffering and cross-bearing, one is not truly following Christ.

1HfKk54cLU9BhXKbUcXZ1JVUUzD0EJ_oj.title: 2017.03.12FLTC_Why We Must Have a Mega Church_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1HfKk54cLU9BhXKbUcXZ1JVUUzD0EJ_oj.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that the church must become a mega church because it is the prophetic destiny spoken over God's house that though the beginning is small, the latter end will greatly increase. He emphasizes that many Christians are deceived into thinking the work of soul winning is finished when in reality hell is filling up and people desperately need salvation through aggressive evangelism and outreach. He calls every church member, shepherd, and leader to consecrate themselves wholly to the mission of seeking and saving the lost, warning that he will not allow anyone to abort the prophetic destiny or change the soul-winning message of the church.

14YdlxqsUzvUDXuZa8DhRoMrB64VsZUsz.title: 2017.04.23FLTC_Why God Wants You To Suffer_Pt.2(Losing, Suffering, Sacrificing, Dying)_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
14YdlxqsUzvUDXuZa8DhRoMrB64VsZUsz.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that God intentionally leads believers into suffering and the wilderness to humble them and prove what is in their hearts, just as God led Israel for 40 years to test their obedience. He explains that suffering is necessary for Christians to learn humility, which is the key to receiving God's grace and promotion, since God resists the proud but gives grace to the humble. He emphasizes that true Christianity requires losing things you love—relationships, friends, clothing styles, entertainment—and that without experiencing this suffering and humility, believers cannot properly obey God or relate compassionately to others.

1o-HiAjkmX6dOJie1CnStKJptPn7HAqaw.title: 2017.07.16FLTC_Formula for Humility Pt.3(Diagnosing Types of Proud Speaking)_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1o-HiAjkmX6dOJie1CnStKJptPn7HAqaw.summary: This transcript contains no actual sermon content from Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills. The entire audio consists of worship songs and music with lyrics about heaven, Jesus's sacrifice, and eternal life. No teaching, scripture exposition, or doctrinal instruction is present in this transcript.

1txI0v9rNK2CLBTw5QucZO1yPfjho4E6z.title: 2017.12.31FLTC_Be Ready For A Long Fight
1txI0v9rNK2CLBTw5QucZO1yPfjho4E6z.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that every prophecy and promise from God requires a long, sustained fight across multiple dimensions—personal battles with self, family conflicts, and leadership struggles—rather than simply being received by faith without action. He emphasizes that Joshua fought for a long time to possess what God had already promised, and similarly believers must be prepared for extended warfare in every area of life including overcoming personal sins, maintaining relationships, and fulfilling ministry callings. The message calls believers to persevere through repeated failures and setbacks, understanding that spiritual maturity and fruitfulness come only through prolonged battle, not instant manifestation.

1DtNzrfGSNurvpiLT7ewDgWH1DlNJkQqk.title: 2017.08.27FLTC_Thos Who Are Ignorant Pt.3
1DtNzrfGSNurvpiLT7ewDgWH1DlNJkQqk.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches on the loyalty of Jesus Christ, emphasizing that believers must follow Christ's example of openly acknowledging His Father and doing only what He sees the Father doing. He warns against the modern church trend of seeking teachers with itching ears who preach prosperity instead of sound doctrine, urging pastors to reprove, rebuke, and exhort with longsuffering regardless of popularity. He stresses the importance of spiritual sons and daughters acknowledging their spiritual fathers and mentors, just as Jesus always acknowledged and honored God the Father despite difficulties.

146mVo3S3q8KJwutSpJ5Y1YoKElpCitGR.title: 2017.09.10FLTC_Your Prophet and Your Miracle_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
146mVo3S3q8KJwutSpJ5Y1YoKElpCitGR.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that receiving miracles is directly connected to how we recognize and honor the prophet, using Jesus' inability to do mighty works in His hometown as the central example of how familiarity breeds unbelief. He emphasizes that people must receive the prophet in the name of a prophet (not as a pastor or teacher) to receive a prophet's reward, and that knowing someone's natural background can create spiritual limitations that block supernatural power. He calls for believers to have a prophetic encounter by properly recognizing the prophetic office and removing the familiarity that prevents miraculous breakthrough in their lives.

1PPPhmE6knwlbtJJIu0IPleSt-GbkODVJ.title: 2017.12.03FLTC_What It Means To Become A Shepherd
topics: Caring for people's needs and wants
1PPPhmE6knwlbtJJIu0IPleSt-GbkODVJ.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that the primary condition for becoming a shepherd is having a genuine desire to serve God and care for people's needs and wants, not perfection or qualifications. He emphasizes that God calls everyone to shepherd at least 8-15 people through prayer and care, arguing that desire and love for God's house—not sinlessness—distinguishes true servants like David who was a murderer yet had a heart after God. He challenges believers to recognize their spiritual desires as God's drawing and to commit to ministry work driven by love for God rather than financial compensation or personal advancement.

1BJ6MJ6-o6Q0GMub0ChprDGXpBAJJ8nYQ.title: 2017.12.24FLTC_What It Means To Become A Shepherd Pt.4(Will It Follow Me)
1BJ6MJ6-o6Q0GMub0ChprDGXpBAJJ8nYQ.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that God gives ministry gifts (apostles, prophets, evangelists, pastors, teachers) specifically to perfect believers for the work of the ministry and building up the body of Christ. He emphasizes that these gifts only operate when believers become captives of Christ under submission to Him, not independent operators. He challenges believers to engage in ministry work that will follow them into eternity, since earthly possessions, careers, and achievements cannot accompany them beyond death—only their works for God will remain.

17AkyBRXP75wg9KnRUr6n4c_3UHQOmsyG.title: 2017.04.30FLTC_Reason_s Why God Wants You to Experience Suffering Pt.2 (Losing, Suffering, Sacrificing, Dying)_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
17AkyBRXP75wg9KnRUr6n4c_3UHQOmsyG.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that God intentionally allows Christians to experience suffering, loss, and sacrifice as a normal part of authentic faith, not as punishment but as evidence of being in God's will. He emphasizes that suffering in the flesh causes believers to cease from sin (1 Peter 4:1) and that endurance through hardship—rather than escape from it—is the Christian calling. He challenges believers to develop a mindset that embraces struggle, rejects the temptation to quit when things are difficult, and understands that every mature Christian must walk through seasons of pain, rejection, and sacrifice.

1J84GoMup8rq0OLGtwSmCwrU4kK4fxsSY.title: 2017.03.19FLTC_Why We Must Build a Mega Church (Jesus Building Project)_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills.output
1J84GoMup8rq0OLGtwSmCwrU4kK4fxsSY.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that Christians who walk after the flesh rather than after the Spirit live condemned lives, and emphasizes that most decisions people make are not led by the Holy Spirit but by routine, culture, or convenience. He argues that the church is Jesus' primary building project, not individuals' personal ambitions, marriages, or careers, and that God blesses those who prioritize soul-winning and church building above their own needs. Using his own testimony of supernatural provision while sacrificing for ministry, he calls the congregation to commit to building a 1,000-seat mega church as part of following the Holy Spirit's leading.

1yJdSOe5jLjIdZkbewfWqK29ymjzWbLMs.title: 2017.05.21FLTC_God_s Letter to Town Church
1yJdSOe5jLjIdZkbewfWqK29ymjzWbLMs.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that God's treasures and anointing are hidden in earthen vessels (prophets and spiritual leaders), and believers must humble themselves through obedience to access these treasures. He emphasizes that prosperity and breakthrough come not through human wisdom or effort, but by believing and honoring God's prophets as instructed in 2 Chronicles 20:20. He challenges believers to pay the price of humility, obedience, and time spent ministering to the Lord in order to receive fresh anointing and break free from limitations.

11Rot5s4n6j_Ky7NRLkb0ulm3v8T763-D.title: 2017.10.08FLTC_The Mystery of Tithing
11Rot5s4n6j_Ky7NRLkb0ulm3v8T763-D.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that Christians possess divine power in multiple forms—power to become sons of God, power to witness, power to be kept, and critically, power to get wealth—but this power only becomes effectual when believers acknowledge what God has placed inside them. He emphasizes that supernatural provision, not natural hard work alone, is the key to prosperity, illustrating how ordinary work (like Peter's fishing) produces extraordinary results when God's power comes upon it. Central to accessing this wealth-creating power is the practice of tithing, which he presents as a key of knowledge that unlocks spiritual doors—honoring God with tithes and first fruits causes God to honor believers with substance and increase in return.

1Z4bZPSUCD4QIIZ0gEHaDl-pWbDOtlqHR.title: 2017.12.17FLTC_What It Means To Become A Shepherd Pt. 3
1Z4bZPSUCD4QIIZ0gEHaDl-pWbDOtlqHR.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that becoming a shepherd requires specific qualifications including tested character, the ability to successfully marry one wife, and being blameless, vigilant, sober, and of good behavior. He emphasizes that church members are like sheep who cannot care for themselves and need shepherds who have been tested through fire to reveal whether they are genuine leaders or pretenders. He also teaches that a leader's wife must be qualified for ministry—grave (honorable), not slanderous (not like Satan as a tempter or adversary), sober, and faithful in all things—because an unqualified wife can disqualify a man from ministry.

1MSeAqdXSDWNcpvBbdRrqEM5DFCk7lCCG.title: 2017.10.22FLTC_Pastor Joshua Heward Mills_Bearing Fruits
1MSeAqdXSDWNcpvBbdRrqEM5DFCk7lCCG.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that every believer must bear fruit after their own kind by reproducing disciples who are like themselves—lawyers winning lawyers, students winning students, professionals winning professionals. He emphasizes that fruitfulness is determined by having the qualities listed in 2 Peter 1:5-8 (diligence, faith, virtue, knowledge, temperance, patience, godliness, brotherly kindness, and charity) actively working and abounding in your life. He warns that those who do not bear fruit will be cut down and cast into the fire, stressing that faith must be demonstrated through works and that Christians must believe in God's power and blessing enough to actively pursue soul winning and church building.

1f1U81pXtkTbGljOvzByGyB0LXr51ByQ0.title: 2017.01.22FLTC_The Art of Following_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1f1U81pXtkTbGljOvzByGyB0LXr51ByQ0.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that copying and following those ahead of you spiritually is a sign of intelligence, not weakness, because it allows you to jump over years of mistakes and research that others have already done. He emphasizes that refusing to follow spiritual fathers and insisting on being original is foolish, comparing it to animals who cannot learn from observation, and argues that every area of life—whether ministry, business, or personal decisions—benefits from imitating proven patterns. He calls believers to follow their spiritual fathers completely, using the principle that 'what has been is what will be' from Ecclesiastes, and warns that independence and originality in spiritual matters leads to repeating avoidable failures.

1JBatBNIH0reL00rt1m7H4n3X1uSBquk0.title: 2017.08.06FLTC_Those Who Are Ignorant
1JBatBNIH0reL00rt1m7H4n3X1uSBquk0.summary: BJosh teaches that Christians will judge angels because despite our spiritual blindness and inability to see spiritual warfare, we overcome Satan through faith while angels fell when tempted. He emphasizes that the Word of God enlightens our eyes like spiritual goggles to identify Satan's work, which Western Christianity often fails to recognize due to moral relativism and emphasis on human rights over biblical truth. He warns that ignorance of Satan's devices gives him advantage over believers, comparing Christians to blind warriors who fight demons they cannot see, which makes their victory miraculous in heaven's eyes.

1idyIFremahmEFTWhmgd8aw1msLR1dLSe.title: 2017.05.14FLTC_You and Your Prophet_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1idyIFremahmEFTWhmgd8aw1msLR1dLSe.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that it is a blessing to be born when there is a prophet, emphasizing that many believers do not recognize or appreciate the spiritual father God has sent to preserve their lives and give them a future. He uses the relationships of Joseph with his brothers and Moses with Aaron to illustrate how God places prophetic voices as fathers who speak instead of God to their people. He stresses that members should honor their prophet because all their blessings, deliverance, and spiritual progress come through the covering and voice God has provided.

1nkEKotDQNCNtbN1GZI5Y4pilsOcYLTbx.title: 2017.03.05FLTC_Reasons Why We Must Be 1000 by December_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1nkEKotDQNCNtbN1GZI5Y4pilsOcYLTbx.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that God knows every detail of each church—their works, labor, patience, and poverty—just as Revelation 2 describes, and therefore members must align their personal vision with God's vision for the church. He emphasizes that Town Church must reach 1000 members by December because having a mega church vision leads members on a spiritual journey of prayer, soul-winning, and faithfulness, increases faith and miracles through greater expectation, and ensures that members can find their marriage partners within the church. He warns that without a clear growth vision, churches become complacent social clubs focused on personal happiness rather than God's purpose, and calls all members—not just leaders—to actively participate in outreach, prayer, and building the church.

1ZhxMN4nFADK1yA-m7tay4rTh-hUwC9n2.title: 2017.06.04FLTC_The Four Types of Wisdom_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1ZhxMN4nFADK1yA-m7tay4rTh-hUwC9n2.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that there are four types of wisdom that direct human decisions: earthly wisdom (which passes away), sensual wisdom (which leads to death), devilish wisdom (which leads to stealing, killing, and destruction), and heavenly wisdom from God (which leads to glory). He emphasizes that every decision we make—including relationships, career, and ministry—is influenced by one of these four wisdoms, and we must recognize which one is directing us. He stresses that following God's wisdom, though mysterious and often not making immediate sense, always leads to glory both in this life and eternally, while following earthly, sensual, or devilish wisdom leads to destruction.

1NsxBi-X56rYD4ZuLKiU7N7EO2QFpZBms.title: 2017.05.07FLTC_Types of Accusations(Those Who Accuse You)_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1NsxBi-X56rYD4ZuLKiU7N7EO2QFpZBms.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches about different types of accusations that believers face, categorizing them as direct allegations, criticisms, and innuendos (accusations by insinuation). He emphasizes that accusations drain spiritual strength and can cause Christians to lose their power and effectiveness in ministry, using examples from Moses, Joseph, and David who all faced false accusations yet remained in God's favor. He warns pastors and believers to recognize these subtle forms of attack and not to be weakened by the various ways people accuse, criticize, or make insinuating remarks about their character or ministry.

19KLk4vczI3Q1jBzYU7gwce1Rf1Ot6fV3.title: 2017.07.30FLTC_120 Reasons Why You Must be a Soul Winner_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
19KLk4vczI3Q1jBzYU7gwce1Rf1Ot6fV3.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that Jesus Christ came specifically to seek and save the lost, not to provide material prosperity or relationships, and this purpose must never be compromised or diluted. He emphasizes that true conversion produces witness and soul winning through the Holy Spirit's power, and Christians who don't witness are devoid of the Spirit regardless of other spiritual experiences like falling down or singing. He challenges believers to remember their own salvation and be grateful enough to become soul winners, arguing that preaching the gospel foolishly is God's ordained method to save people who cannot find salvation through human wisdom.

1tFv0o0yenfYKx4dCDW47Wn0R_xEmKm3Y.title: 2017.06.18FLTC_The Art of Following Pt.2(The Ability to Recognise Spiritual People)_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: relating to people in the spirit not the flesh
David's recognition of Saul as anointed
not living for ourselves but for Christ
1tFv0o0yenfYKx4dCDW47Wn0R_xEmKm3Y.summary: BJosh teaches that one of David's keys to success was his ability to recognize and relate to people according to their spiritual identity rather than their fleshly appearance or behavior, as seen when David refused to kill Saul despite Saul's murderous pursuit because he recognized him as God's anointed. He emphasizes that since Christ died, believers must discern the spiritual reality of people—especially fathers and spiritual leaders—beyond their weaknesses, failures, or outward circumstances in the flesh. Using examples like Maria Woodworth-Etter (a divorced remarried woman powerfully used by God) and the contrast between Jesus and Barabbas, he warns that failure to recognize spiritual authority and honor spiritual fathers brings curses, while spiritual discernment unlocks blessing.

1hi-GfqY90i3zMXaPiDnklafQJp_qImP2.title: 2017.04.09FLTC_Why God Wants You to Suffer (Losing, Suffering, Sacrificing, Dying)_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1hi-GfqY90i3zMXaPiDnklafQJp_qImP2.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that God uses suffering as the primary tool to teach His people obedience, explaining that even Jesus had to learn obedience through suffering despite being the Son of God. He emphasizes that pain, rejection, and difficult experiences serve as divine guidance that leads believers to understand and follow God's perfect will rather than just His good or acceptable will. He concludes that suffering is not punishment but preparation, as God uses painful experiences to guide believers into glory, referencing Psalm 73:24 that after divine counsel and obedience comes glory.

1mgDx3CpD0OXQHLm83pSAciaZFUlP-scP.title: 2017.10.15FLTC_Bearing Fruits (Diligence)_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1mgDx3CpD0OXQHLm83pSAciaZFUlP-scP.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that Christians are ordained by God to bear fruit, meaning to spiritually reproduce by winning souls and leading people to Christ, not merely to worship. He emphasizes that fruitfulness is the proof of genuine Christianity, just as physical birth proves biological identity, and that barrenness in soul-winning reveals a lack of essential qualities. He stresses that diligence is the foundational quality needed for both spiritual fruitfulness and natural prosperity, and that the same qualities required to bear spiritual fruit are what make people successful in every area of life.

1bV-kIZLNKUZVRDGQxNZbgTOHADot4nh0.title: 2017.02.19FLTC_Loyalty and Disloyalty (Critical Stage)_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1bV-kIZLNKUZVRDGQxNZbgTOHADot4nh0.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that the fourth stage of disloyalty is the critical stage, where people begin to criticize and accuse their leaders, family members, or others they serve. He emphasizes that criticism and accusation are characteristics of Satan (the accuser of the brethren) and bring curses upon those who practice them, using Miriam and Aaron's criticism of Moses as an example that resulted in Miriam becoming leprous. He warns that faithfulness is required in all areas of life—not just in church but in family, work, and relationships—and Christians must stop speaking critical words because God judges accusers and such speech can bring destructive consequences into their lives.

1yUrf1oZWW8SZBE1MntBlOtNizSJZip9v.title: 2017.09.03FLTC_Proton Introdurction Pt.1_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: Milk versus meat in God's Word
1yUrf1oZWW8SZBE1MntBlOtNizSJZip9v.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that seeking God's kingdom first means prioritizing church work and ministry early in life, because God needs young people and the kingdom requires an early start. He explains that Scripture has levels of depth—from milk (basic verses) to butter to oil to deep waters—and Christians must progress beyond surface-level Bible reading to experience revelation, anointing, and the miraculous works of God. He emphasizes that all Scripture is God-breathed and profitable for doctrine, reproof, correction, and instruction in righteousness, equipping believers to be complete and furnished for every good work.

1w1omkyRvPXkT5FJhkRpFDetsTG7aDTkL.title: 2017.11.12FLTC_Pastor Joshua Heward-Mills_Why You Must Be A Part Of A Mega Church
1w1omkyRvPXkT5FJhkRpFDetsTG7aDTkL.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that believers must be part of a mega church because large gatherings create expectation which releases the baptism and power of the Holy Spirit. He emphasizes that in a mega church there are more miracles and manifestations of the Spirit because there is more collective faith when many believers gather together. He demonstrates the nine gifts of the Spirit from 1 Corinthians 12, including words of knowledge, prophecy, and tongues, showing that these manifestations are given so believers can profit and experience God's supernatural provision and breakthrough.

1Wz_pxTkg5Z6pVmQxDtTiuAFlOshh2GEJ.title: 2017.12.10FLTC_What It Means To Become A Shepherd Pt. 2
1Wz_pxTkg5Z6pVmQxDtTiuAFlOshh2GEJ.summary: Pastor BJosh teaches that becoming a shepherd is a natural stage of Christian growth that every believer should reach, just as children naturally mature and no longer need only milk. He emphasizes that all you need to become a shepherd is desire, and explains that shepherding means being a caring, loving guide who says 'let us go into the house of the Lord' and prays for others. He stresses there is a great need for shepherds (not just miracle workers), and that God will bless the latter end of those who take up shepherding despite their own problems, just as He did with Job.

1yPkm2fngA6uIOWkkVXwwcoypN6IxCHtL.title: 2017.07.02FLTC_Formula for Humility Pt.1(Four Types of Proud Speakings)_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1yPkm2fngA6uIOWkkVXwwcoypN6IxCHtL.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that God hates a proud heart and that what comes out of a person's mouth reveals what is truly in their heart, especially under pressure or emotional situations. He emphasizes that all power, ability, and position comes from God alone, warning against overconfident declarations that suggest self-sufficiency or independence from God. He illustrates through Pilate's interaction with Jesus that recognizing our dependence on God and our need for Him in every area of life is the foundation of true humility.

1Cppi7Uhr-ZjK_Yp9rhG7d978oh7vmX77.title: 2017.10.01FLTC_The Prophets Honour_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
topics: life determined by who you meet
turning hearts of children to fathers
1Cppi7Uhr-ZjK_Yp9rhG7d978oh7vmX77.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that pastors and prophets are ordained by God from among men specifically for things pertaining to God, not to solve every earthly problem, and must be honored both with material gifts and with respectful words. He emphasizes that your entire life destiny is determined by who you meet and how you receive them, using Peter's immediate response to Jesus' call as the pivotal moment that placed him on a throne forever versus the unnamed man who rejected Jesus. He stresses that honoring spiritual fathers goes beyond mere obedience and includes turning your heart toward them, which carries promises of blessing and long life, while rejection brings curses.

1nM9tpb18j_7ryYoRLLxbDyzxkAinUr_Z.title: 2017.10.29FLTC_Pastor Joshua Heward-Mills_Bearing Fruits Pt.3
1nM9tpb18j_7ryYoRLLxbDyzxkAinUr_Z.summary: Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills teaches that Christians are known by their spiritual fruit, not by external appearances, and that fruitfulness requires specific internal qualities listed in 2 Peter 1:8. He explains that lack of knowledge, self-control, godliness, brotherly kindness, and love are primary reasons why Christians remain unfruitful in their lives and ministries. He emphasizes that the greatest enemies of fruitfulness are the cares of this world, the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things that choke the word and make believers unproductive.

1nsyffaey6Ys0jKqGAiuKoyhe0IrLut32.title: 2017.05.28FLTC_God_s Letter To Town Church Pt.2 (Grace and Peace)_Bishop Dag Heward-Mills
1nsyffaey6Ys0jKqGAiuKoyhe0IrLut32.summary: Bishop Dag teaches that grace and peace from God are the two essential things believers need throughout their lives, emphasizing that human weakness and evil nature require God's continual grace. He explains that God has adopted believers as His children before the foundation of the world, making them accepted in the beloved (the church), which is the primary place of acceptance despite life's inevitable rejections. He emphasizes that God abundantly gives wisdom and prudence through Scripture and books, revealing the mysterious will of God through prayer and study of His Word.

1BhUvxiu8W1J-aA5gLpNqHSx9yow3R4jx.title: 2017.11.26FLTC_Why You Must Be Part Of A Mega Church Pt 3
topics: Prince of the Power of the Air
1BhUvxiu8W1J-aA5gLpNqHSx9yow3R4jx.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that in a mega church there is greater power to confront the prince of the power of the air and cast out demons that cause sickness, depression, and destruction in people's lives. He explains that evil spirits (nephilim, demons from fallen angels) have infiltrated humanity since Genesis and that Jesus's ministry was characterized by casting out devils and healing the sick. He declares that through the name of Jesus and faith in that name, believers in the mega church can receive deliverance from every demonic influence, genetic curse, chronic disease, and oppressive spirit.

1tj3lVJaXOH-u4ugXEZXX5F3MY3N7wmoT.title: 2017.12.03FLC_How To Be A Humble Minister Like Jesus Pt. 1
1tj3lVJaXOH-u4ugXEZXX5F3MY3N7wmoT.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that Jesus' most important lesson for ministers is not His miracles or mega-church success, but His humility, as demonstrated when He joined the baptism line and humbled Himself to become a man and die. He explains that God gives grace (supernatural help) only to the humble, and that humility can override the law of divine selection, as seen when Jesus healed the Syrophoenician woman and the Centurion who demonstrated exceptional humility. He warns that pride blocks earthly and spiritual blessings, calling believers to remain teachable like children and to embrace servant roles rather than seeking status or being "too big" for menial tasks in church.

1bFuygr6CB3jyj0Pt9xKjiFoaSoWn7LxK.title: 2017.03.12FLC_HYPService_Honour Your Prophet_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1bFuygr6CB3jyj0Pt9xKjiFoaSoWn7LxK.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that believers must receive and honor their prophet at the rank and level he operates in, not merely as a teacher or preacher, in order to receive the prophet's reward and full anointing. He emphasizes that the level of power and blessing released in someone's life is directly connected to the level of honor they give to the man of God, illustrated by the Shunammite woman who progressively increased her honor and received miraculous blessing. He warns that lack of recognition and honor for the anointing limits the prophet's ability to perform mighty works, just as Jesus could do no mighty works in his hometown due to their familiarity and lack of honor.

1BgjPXzaZGg0lk8HIJLc0T-R6MDO-qiOX.title: 2017.04.30FLC_Those Who Accuse You Pt.1_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1BgjPXzaZGg0lk8HIJLc0T-R6MDO-qiOX.summary: Pastor Joshua teaches that Satan's most effective weapon against believers is accusation rather than frontal attack, and that accusations weaken us by being partly true and destroying our confidence, strength, and relationships. He emphasizes that just as Jesus refused to condemn the woman caught in adultery, no one with sin has the right to accuse others, and believers must learn to identify and cast down the spirit of accusation operating within the church. He explains that when accusations are allowed to operate, they steal salvation, strength, and the power of Christ from believers' lives, leaving them discouraged and isolated.

1AIl4o-th49qNDfMaR4YhgVHW98quXL4C.title: 2017.08.27FLC_Tell Them Pt. 2 (The Love Of Jesus Christ)_Pastor Joshua Heward-Mills
1AIl4o-th49qNDfMaR4YhgVHW98quXL4C.summary: BJosh teaches that Christians must become soul-winners because without the shedding of Jesus' blood there is no forgiveness of sins, and Jesus demonstrated the greatest love by laying down His life. He systematically compares Jesus' love against four major types of human love—brotherly love, patriotic love, maternal love, and romantic love—showing how each falls short and is conditional or limited. Jesus' love stands alone as the greatest because it is unconditional, sacrificial, and directed toward sinners who could give nothing back, proving that no love in human history compares to Christ's redemptive love.

1rFj3uy9s8MHW6wm2haUTZbN3pmPfYbli.title: 2017.09.24FLC_The Disguises of the Prophet_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1rFj3uy9s8MHW6wm2haUTZbN3pmPfYbli.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that receiving a prophet in the name of a prophet—not as a teacher, advisor, or righteous man—unlocks a specific prophet's reward including deliverance from Egypt (bondage, failure, servitude) and supernatural preservation of blessings. He emphasizes that by a prophet God brought Israel out of Egypt and by a prophet they were preserved, meaning prophets carry unique anointing to break cycles of failure and maintain breakthrough in believers' lives. Christians must identify and honor prophets correctly with high levels of belief rather than discussion or debate, as different spiritual offices carry different rewards and blessings.

1Ph_gaeJIKCeD12t0G9zn0votoAvi-gcO.title: 2017.06.25FLC_The Wisdom of the Serpent(Wise as Serpents)_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1Ph_gaeJIKCeD12t0G9zn0votoAvi-gcO.summary: Pastor Joshua teaches that Christians must give themselves wholly (100%) to God and His work, using the wisdom of the serpent which commits its entire body to capturing prey as an illustration. He emphasizes that profiting in Christianity only appears when believers stop dividing their attention between the Kingdom and worldly concerns, demonstrating complete commitment with their mind, heart, and soul. He challenges church members to prioritize soul-winning and building God's church above their personal needs, asserting that Matthew 6:33 only works when believers truly seek God's kingdom first with undivided devotion.

1WGqgb1zIQa177Y2mF9gNRue3os-S2CN_.title: 2017.12.10FLC_How To Be A Humble Minister Like Jesus Pt. 2
1WGqgb1zIQa177Y2mF9gNRue3os-S2CN_.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that pride has many surprising sources including perfection, beauty, wisdom, riches, knowledge, prayer, fasting, tithing, and even dreams and revelations. He emphasizes that Jesus Christ is the ultimate teacher of humility, instructing believers to learn from Him by being meek and lowly, choosing the lowest room, and not doing their own will but God's will. He warns that proud people always want to do what they want rather than submitting to spiritual authority, and calls believers to humble themselves before God lifts them up.

1vv225WnJCQDPAYKUbeYBgx0aGno1Tr-P.title: 2017.08.20FLC_Tell Them (Without the shedding of Blood)
1vv225WnJCQDPAYKUbeYBgx0aGno1Tr-P.summary: Bishop Josh teaches that without the shedding of Jesus' blood there is absolutely no remission of sins, and no amount of good works, songs, church attendance, or blessings can wash away sin. He emphasizes that we must be soul winners because the blood of Jesus must reach people through our witness, just as it reached us through someone who told us the gospel. He urges believers to understand their responsibility to evangelize because people are dying without salvation, and only the blood of Jesus—not human effort or religiosity—can convert sinners and grant them eternal life.

1PRYFUSeqDYYQ-PRcPzuQPBCYPCtwt4LM.title: 2017.01.22FLC_Bebelos_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1PRYFUSeqDYYQ-PRcPzuQPBCYPCtwt4LM.summary: Pastor Joshua teaches on the Greek word 'bebelos' (profane) from Hebrews 12:16, explaining that it describes someone like Esau who despises spiritual things, disregards holy things, and disrespects their God-given birthright. He emphasizes that understanding the original Greek word reveals deeper meaning and power lost in English translation, showing how bebelos means not just profane but someone who treats sacred things with contempt. He warns that believers can become bebelos by despising what God has given them, allowing outsiders (like Ruth the Moabite) to take the blessings intended for those who were originally positioned to receive them.

1QTV8kiSL4IpxWBkelqtULg_iseslH6l6.title: 2017.05.07FLC_Those Who Accuse You Pt.2_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills
1QTV8kiSL4IpxWBkelqtULg_iseslH6l6.summary: Pastor Joshua teaches that accusations are a primary weapon of Satan against believers and the church, and that when the accuser is present, salvation, strength, the kingdom of God, and the power of Christ are hindered. He reveals hidden laws of accusation, particularly that people are typically accused of the exact opposite of what they truly are or are trying to do, citing examples like Moses being accused of pride when he was the meekest man, and Jesus being accused of destroying the temple when He came to build His church. He emphasizes that believers must recognize accusations as devices of the enemy, reject them with strength, and understand that these false charges are attempts to stop God's work and undermine their calling.

1SD81PTVP2jiBbvJlXso25aGkoaq54Xdv.title: 2017.03.19FLC_In the Atmosphere of War_Ps Joshua Heward-Mills.output
1SD81PTVP2jiBbvJlXso25aGkoaq54Xdv.summary: Pastor Joshua teaches that Christianity is not about peace and comfort but is actually a war that believers must fight while dealing with broken families, poverty, sickness, and pain. He emphasizes that Christians must continue serving God, paying tithes, attending church, and doing ministry even when their personal lives are falling apart and their spiritual armor is damaged. Using examples like John Wesley's troubled marriage and Charles Spurgeon's kidney stones, he insists that believers must stand and function in the atmosphere of war rather than waiting for everything to be okay before they serve God.

1dE-z51paD98aEBfQoWBoMKMNck-55PWj.title: 2017.09.03FLC_Tell them pt.3
1dE-z51paD98aEBfQoWBoMKMNck-55PWj.summary: This transcript does not contain a sermon by Bishop Joshua Heward-Mills, but rather testimonies from Bible school students during an International Sunday service. Two students shared their testimonies: Talent from Zimbabwe testified about divine healing from a serious head injury that could have caused brain damage, and Wendy from Namibia shared her salvation testimony and journey to Bible school after being invited to church. The focus is on thanksgiving for Bishop Heward-Mills' ministry of church planting in various nations and the opportunities provided through Bible school training.

1OHbgB_Oj2Dz-C0-fR2sQhB744wr3nVTP.title: 01 Indwelling of the Spirit
topics: Spirit poured out from on high
Holy Spirit within, with, and upon
1OHbgB_Oj2Dz-C0-fR2sQhB744wr3nVTP.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that the primary reason for lack of church growth and ministerial effectiveness is the absence of the Holy Spirit being poured out on leaders and members. He emphasizes three ways the Holy Spirit interacts with believers: within (indwelling), with (presence), and upon (anointing for service), explaining that the Spirit within never occurred in the Old Testament but is available now. He testifies that every area of his life—from academics to ministry to overcoming depression and worldliness—was transformed when the Holy Spirit was poured out on him, and calls for the same outpouring on his governance team and church.

1RKcBbHAPadg4bvdq4DKNEtc6Tq53HaNq.title: 07.14 - 10 Reasons Every Christian Sh
1RKcBbHAPadg4bvdq4DKNEtc6Tq53HaNq.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that prayer is the fundamental sign of faith and that without faith it is impossible to please God, requiring believers to both believe that God exists and that He rewards those who diligently seek Him. He argues that Christians often fail to pray because they lack genuine faith in prayer's effectiveness, trusting more in their own efforts, connections, and strategies than in God's power to answer persistent prayer. He emphasizes that wisdom, guidance, and breakthrough come only through dedicated prayer in the secret place, not through education or human effort alone, and calls believers to pursue God with the same diligence they apply to worldly pursuits.

1AoGm6-HCYMgogtoJMsJ3YlEZLBrGJwN2.title: 12.22 - Humility and Pride
1AoGm6-HCYMgogtoJMsJ3YlEZLBrGJwN2.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that pride is the only cause of contention and quarrels in relationships, and that it prevents people from receiving earthly blessings like jobs, marriages, and prosperity because proud people refuse to accept their current state and do menial work. He emphasizes that humility is demonstrated through two characteristics Jesus gave: behaving like a child and behaving like a servant, particularly being willing to do lowly, menial jobs without complaint. True humility brings wisdom, guidance from God, and eventual promotion, as exemplified by Jesus' disciples who served as "house help" for three years before becoming apostles.

1Fjqg18s7lGdkljJRq9n7Q593pBNe1TEf.title: 10.13 - Honour Relationships
1Fjqg18s7lGdkljJRq9n7Q593pBNe1TEf.summary: BJosh teaches that Christians must honor their relationship with the Holy Spirit who dwells in and operates through God's anointed servants, warning that those who despise or dishonor prophets and pastors are actually touching God Himself and will face severe consequences. He emphasizes that God gave humanity over to reprobate minds when they did not retain Him in their knowledge, as seen in Western society's moral decline, and stresses that spiritual blessings and breakthroughs come through honoring prophets with offerings and respect. He concludes that the church must honor their prophet annually with seed offerings because without controversy, the lesser is blessed by the greater, and those who receive a prophet in the name of a prophet receive a prophet's reward.

1rZP34354qWwkJM96rQWIQkRpQHdw4F-G.title: 02.17 - The Global Curses - Neutralis
1rZP34354qWwkJM96rQWIQkRpQHdw4F-G.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that there are three major global curses operating in the world: the curse on Adam (sweat, toil, and death), the curse on Eve (sorrow in childbirth and desire for husband), and the curse of Noah on Ham (servanthood and inability to build or own). He explains that these curses affect all humanity globally and are evident in struggles with work, relationships, childbearing, and systemic poverty. The way to overcome these curses is through faith in the world to come—the New Jerusalem—where there will be no more curse, no death, no sun, and believers will live in glory forever with resurrected bodies and eternal rewards.

1ZhGt2LlYVTT5f0m3zY4uLDw00746W1ob.title: 11.03 - How to Become a Mature Christ
1ZhGt2LlYVTT5f0m3zY4uLDw00746W1ob.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that spiritual maturity comes from understanding the truth of God's Word, which transforms believers from baby Christians who don't understand why they do things into mature adults who cannot be tossed by every doctrine. He emphasizes that Christians must be visibly different from unbelievers—a peculiar people zealous of good works—evidenced by changed lifestyle, music, relationships, and obedience to practices like tithing that open heaven's provision. He warns against unequal yoking with unbelievers in marriage or close friendships because they carry different burdens and directions, making it impossible to walk together in God's purposes.

1haWNU8SXOHvoqCLBjzEputeJHkZTCkwK.title: 01.20 - Why You must pray
1haWNU8SXOHvoqCLBjzEputeJHkZTCkwK.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that prayer transforms believers into people of spiritual authority who can overcome the demonic entities (fallen angels, jumping angels, and demons) that fill the world with wickedness. He emphasizes that prayer is not about getting things from God but about developing closeness and intimate fellowship with Him, particularly through the Holy Spirit's communion. He argues that without consistent prayer—including extended hours in God's presence—Christians will lack the authority needed to control their lives and will be overpowered by spiritual forces.

1QI9mvs-iX8r_sSqoRQBcxTzHBDFJe60S.title: 01.13 - Victory Secrets - The secret
1QI9mvs-iX8r_sSqoRQBcxTzHBDFJe60S.summary: BJosh teaches that sacrifice is the secret to receiving power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and blessings, using Jesus as the Lamb who was slain as the primary example of how sacrifice releases God's power. He emphasizes that Christians must literally leave behind former friends, relationships with unbelievers, fornication, old entertainment, and worldly habits—not just attend church—because transformation requires actual loss and separation. He argues the modern church lacks power because it has abandoned the message of daily sacrifice, including extended prayer, breaking up with ungodly relationships, and walking away from the old life to receive manifold blessings in return.

1oPyCi8irfPqQdU-E3G6JnIaZpO6M6PiN.title: 08.18 - Don_t Be Surprised That You M
1oPyCi8irfPqQdU-E3G6JnIaZpO6M6PiN.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that being born again requires first acknowledging that you are fundamentally wrong and sinful, not just different or misunderstood. He emphasizes that like Adam and Eve, humans naturally refuse to admit fault and instead justify themselves by focusing on their good deeds (like the Pharisee who fasted and tithed) rather than confessing their sins (like the publican who cried for mercy). He argues that many church attendees have never truly been born again because they've never genuinely confessed they are sinners who need complete spiritual regeneration, not just religious activity or self-improvement.

1VrwLG3bofYHMmBYrf9OAk1JGgzOeDFPm.title: 11.24 - Those Who Forget
1VrwLG3bofYHMmBYrf9OAk1JGgzOeDFPm.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that leaving the church results in two major tragedies: losing your position (as positions are given to the faithful who remain stable) and entering into poverty and desolation (despite outward symbols of wealth). Using the parable of the prodigal son, he emphasizes that God places people in specific churches through messengers/interpreters (one among a thousand) and that walking out on the church is walking out on God, not just a pastor. He warns that faithfulness and staying where God has planted you is essential for spiritual prosperity, guidance, and fulfilling your divine calling, while leaving leads to wasted potential and loss of inheritance.

1_N171EYT7IHviy_aovelvb-kjTvKvz7F.title: 07.07 - The Art of Following David Pt
1_N171EYT7IHviy_aovelvb-kjTvKvz7F.summary: BJosh teaches that following David means learning the art of serving and blessing others rather than being self-absorbed and only seeking blessings for yourself. He emphasizes that true prosperity comes through faith and patience combined with generosity and ministry to others, not through selfish receiving. He also teaches that when people reject you, like David's brother rejected him, you must turn away and move on to find those who believe in you rather than staying stuck trying to convince those who won't accept you.

1DSOwwCg8nWAuHSIocKlXUzomOcDiIpsK.title: 06.30 - The Art of Following David Pt
1DSOwwCg8nWAuHSIocKlXUzomOcDiIpsK.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that true success comes from starting at the bottom with menial jobs and working your way up with humility, contrasting David who remained humble after being anointed with Jehu who immediately seized the throne. He emphasizes that believers must operate by biblical wisdom rather than worldly wisdom, learning to humble themselves and serve faithfully in small things before being promoted. He stresses that coming from a poor background or receiving unearned status leads to a destructive mindset, whereas working through hardship builds character and the ability to handle promotion when it comes.
1DSOwwCg8nWAuHSIocKlXUzomOcDiIpsK.keyMessage: The pathway to legitimate authority and lasting success is through humble service in small things, not through pride, shortcuts, or inherited status.

1n9v8AnAu9FVHynZRbG_VuHzHqQDKnbx1.title: 06.23 - The Art of Following David
topics: Inheriting promises through faith and patience
Despising the day of small things
1n9v8AnAu9FVHynZRbG_VuHzHqQDKnbx1.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that God promotes people who respect and are faithful in small beginnings, using David's life as the primary example of someone who rose from insignificance to greatness. He emphasizes that faithfulness with little things—whether small ministries, money, or another person's work—is the requirement for God to give you your own ministry and blessings. He warns that despising small things, being unfaithful with tithes and offerings, or failing to honor small responsibilities will prevent believers from receiving true riches and their own God-given assignments.

18HNERqmjC4J9TphO0MsbRQpuNpg_SRHw.title: 03.10 - The Church and Barrenness - C
18HNERqmjC4J9TphO0MsbRQpuNpg_SRHw.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that the primary purpose of every Christian is to bear spiritual fruit by winning souls and making disciples, just as God chose believers specifically to go forth and bring forth fruit. He warns that many Christians appear spiritually active through worship, prayer, and church involvement, but remain barren because they fail to engage in evangelism and produce converts. Using the Hebrew words 'acar' and 'shakal' for barrenness, he emphasizes that a beautiful church with good worship but no soul-winning is like barren ground—the reproductive part is not functioning regardless of how pleasant everything else appears.

1cChwDF5SG-DP5CC9ojxdCnEO2MGlpJvR.title: 03.03 - The Church and Barrenness - C
1cChwDF5SG-DP5CC9ojxdCnEO2MGlpJvR.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that spiritual barrenness occurs when Christians lack the reproductive capacity to birth new believers, comparing it to the Hebrew word 'akar' which means removal of reproductive organs. He emphasizes that while Christians may be active in worship, singing, dancing, and church attendance, if they are not witnessing and doing evangelism—the 'reproductive part' of Christianity—they are spiritually barren and strain their relationship with Christ. Using Ezekiel 33:30-33, he warns that many Christians listen to preaching with no intention of acting on it, and urges believers to use their spiritual reproductive capacity through soul-winning and outreach before they die.

1R8MtEZ7fHMr5TdTj-rwQQjmGwC0Nie3W.title: 11.17 - How to Become a Mature Christ
1R8MtEZ7fHMr5TdTj-rwQQjmGwC0Nie3W.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that mature Christians must befriend sinners to fulfill the Great Commission, rejecting the church's tendency to judge and exclude those who are broken, because salvation itself is God's gift of persuasion, not human achievement. He emphasizes that speaking in tongues is essential for direct fellowship with God, allowing believers to pray according to God's will and build a personal relationship with Him beyond religious rules and head knowledge. The central message is that Christianity is fundamentally about having intimate fellowship with God through the Holy Spirit, not merely following religious principles or maintaining external holiness.

1_Vwamrdc0S3-PxDnE605HKqrwn3DV_0m.title: 09.29 - Honour Your Prophet
1_Vwamrdc0S3-PxDnE605HKqrwn3DV_0m.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that believers must recognize and honor the prophets and spiritual authorities God has sent into their lives, particularly by acknowledging the spiritual work done for their salvation and transformation. He emphasizes that honor is not just verbal appreciation but requires substance (material giving), establishing 'Honor Your Prophet Day' as an institutionalized time when this honor is due, similar to how people celebrate birthdays or Valentine's Day. He argues that true gratitude begins with recognizing what has actually been done for you spiritually, comparing Mary Magdalene's grateful worship of Jesus with her alabaster box to how believers should honor those through whom they received salvation and spiritual knowledge.

1zmARBWuZ2ORajra-umqyrFHd06ZOFYxy.title: 10.20 - Honour and the Anointing
1zmARBWuZ2ORajra-umqyrFHd06ZOFYxy.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that honor—especially toward those who teach you the Word of God—is the key that unlocks the power and anointing of God in a person's life. He argues that the curse of Ham (dishonoring his father Noah) explains Africa's poverty and struggles, and that without honor for prophets and pastors, believers will never experience supernatural breakthroughs despite hard work or education. He emphasizes that God releases His power through human vessels (prophets, pastors, teachers), and only those who recognize and honor these anointed leaders will see miracles, provision, and transformation in their lives.

1iSn8A5IVKUCs3d3Q8dHjWNHFlwlG6h1G.title: 05.12 - The New Creation Pt.3
1iSn8A5IVKUCs3d3Q8dHjWNHFlwlG6h1G.summary: BJosh teaches that when a person is born again, they become the righteousness of God in Christ—as holy and perfect as Jesus Himself—not through works but through faith, just as Abraham's belief was counted as righteousness. He emphasizes that believers must "put off the old man" (their former sinful identity and behaviors) and "put on the new man" which is created in true righteousness and holiness, recognizing that their spirit has been transformed even though their flesh may still feel the same. Christians must actively live out their new identity by changing their friends, habits, and lifestyle to align with who they have become in Christ, understanding that their righteousness is imputed (installed like software) and enables them to reign in life, pray boldly, and overcome the world.

1DKiHhH3GvLsjTltuHnF9fr5erf2S0Qzz.title: 03.31 - 15 Essential Features of a Sh
1DKiHhH3GvLsjTltuHnF9fr5erf2S0Qzz.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that shepherds must first develop a personal relationship with God because no human can solve the deep crises that arise in ministry, and knowing God through prayer and solitude is essential for waiting on Him. He emphasizes that aspiring ministers must cultivate the habit of listening to preaching and watching sermon videos, as you cannot become something you have never listened to or learned from. He stresses that a personal fasting life and various forms of self-denial are crucial spiritual disciplines for anyone called to serve God and shepherd His people.

1VZNFPLxDdMAXY1VxW3nrHZjtY8O978f-.title: 03.24 - The Church and Barrenness - C
1VZNFPLxDdMAXY1VxW3nrHZjtY8O978f-.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches on spiritual barrenness, specifically the 'tsia' type which means dryness in the spirit, using Isaiah 41:18 to explain how God transforms dry wilderness into pools of water through His presence. He describes three progressive levels of God's presence as water: the well (for personal needs), the river (blessing others), and the rain (widespread blessing), emphasizing that many Christians and churches suffer from spiritual dryness characterized by absence of God's presence. He critiques modern worship that focuses on materialistic requests rather than genuine worship, calling believers to pursue intimacy with God's presence above earthly blessings and to understand they need eternal inheritance in heaven beyond their temporal earthly existence.

1fsOrxCb1SZQzVkW4c3rbptG7j49gd5pk.title: 07.21 - How to Pray
1fsOrxCb1SZQzVkW4c3rbptG7j49gd5pk.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that prayer transforms Christians into people of spiritual authority over demons and diseases, while prayerlessness leads to fighting, lust, quarreling, and vulnerability to demonic influence. He demonstrates through the crucifixion of Christ how demons orchestrated Jesus' betrayal through prayerless people like Judas, the Pharisees, and the crowds who shouted for Barabbas, showing that similar demonic activity causes Christians to abandon their leaders and churches today. He emphasizes that Christians must maintain consistent prayer lives to receive authority from God, resist demonic manipulation, and avoid the unfaithfulness and treachery that characterizes Satan's work.

11yaSqWkoMMp4pKUnnin-i2PRpUxWDyGu.title: 10.27 - Why Christians Do Not Use The
11yaSqWkoMMp4pKUnnin-i2PRpUxWDyGu.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches on the Parable of the Talents from Matthew 25, emphasizing that every Christian has been given talents and gifts by God to use for His kingdom work, not to hide or hoard. He confronts three main reasons Christians don't use their talents: hiding their abilities out of selfishness, accusing and criticizing church leaders instead of serving, and operating in fear or laziness. He warns that all believers will stand alone before God to give account of how they used what He entrusted to them, urging the congregation to actively serve in practical church ministries rather than passively attending services.

1nZbUOAe9ZPIsdT0Oc_Qs7etf5Yy26er4.title: 08.25 - Many Are Called
1nZbUOAe9ZPIsdT0Oc_Qs7etf5Yy26er4.summary: BJosh teaches that God specifically calls failures and people who have tried and failed at various things in life, using Moses as the primary example of someone who failed but was still called by God. He emphasizes that one of the key characteristics of called people is that they are failures—whether in school, relationships, ministry attempts, or holiness—and God wants to use them despite their failures. He concludes that as believers engage in God's work and soul winning, God justifies them and transforms their character in the process.

1NjNWdFfF64u1bOHo-TtY3Gmxm5mCZKE4.title: 06.02 - The New Creation Pt. 6
1NjNWdFfF64u1bOHo-TtY3Gmxm5mCZKE4.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that after salvation, while your spirit is made righteous and holy, your body (flesh) remains unchanged and must be kept under control through the Holy Spirit's power. He emphasizes that Christians must actively thirst for and seek the Holy Spirit's companionship to hear His voice for guidance, provision, and direction in daily life, as illustrated by biblical examples like Philip being told to join the Ethiopian's chariot. He warns that even spiritually mature believers can fall into sin if they don't deliberately subjugate their flesh, using the example of angels who left heaven for earthly pleasures, and stresses that repentance requires concrete action like removing all connections to sin.

1S3XNSxwxCRKQ-TprrmorE7RtCJ2jEBER.title: 12.29 - Thanksgiving
1S3XNSxwxCRKQ-TprrmorE7RtCJ2jEBER.summary: BJosh teaches that true thanksgiving requires understanding and accepting God's will even when prayers go unanswered and circumstances remain difficult, emphasizing we should give thanks "in all things" not "for all things." He uses the story of the ten lepers to show that only one returned to thank Jesus despite being healed of just one problem out of many, and that this gratitude demonstrated faith that perfected everything else in his life. BJosh challenges the congregation to turn around and acknowledge what God has done in 2019 despite incomplete solutions, imperfect circumstances, and ongoing struggles, because thanksgiving itself is an act of faith that pleases God.

1PdLcYtErMdJaCcrjBYQn0m7DsDiLbBKA.title: 02.24 - The Curse of Eve- Neutralisin
1PdLcYtErMdJaCcrjBYQn0m7DsDiLbBKA.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that the curse of Eve in Genesis 3:16 contains four specific curses: multiplied sorrow, pain in childbirth, desire for husband, and male rule over women, all of which are actively working in women's lives today as evidenced by statistics on mental health, grief, and male dominance. He explains that curses are powerful pronouncements with far-reaching consequences that surround a person's life, requiring believers to actively bind and reject negative words spoken against them. The key to overcoming or reducing the effects of these curses is through wisdom, understanding, and submission to God's Word, not through human effort or fighting against divinely established authority.

1E6b0Jn12RRid6ZJ9kiKiNGAxnP1h6GIO.title: 11.03 - Birthday Prayer
1E6b0Jn12RRid6ZJ9kiKiNGAxnP1h6GIO.summary: Bishop Joshua prays a prophetic birthday blessing over Joshua, asking God for wisdom beyond his years and the ability to survive and stand where others have fallen. He invokes spiritual warfare language, loosing angels to fight against those who fight against Joshua and binding Satan, devils, and wicked spirits that seek to devour the 'man child' as referenced in Revelation imagery. He declares protection against premature death, accidents, and enemy conspiracies, pronouncing blessings of survival, prevailing, and multiplying ten thousand times more.

15nYPFQcjuhjyw2I-xG3tbT7RNoSUWr6W.title: 08.11 - Reasons Why Every Christian S
15nYPFQcjuhjyw2I-xG3tbT7RNoSUWr6W.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that prayer possesses creative power to birth entirely new dimensions and possibilities in a Christian's life that their background, education, or natural abilities cannot access. He emphasizes that spiritual advancement and breakthrough—whether in ministry growth, personal holiness, provision, or relationships—only comes through prayer, not through fleshly efforts or human strategies. Using Isaiah 66:8 as his foundation, he explains that just as Zion travailed in prayer and suddenly brought forth children, Christians must pray to create new realities and break into higher dimensions that seem impossible or out of reach.

1keCkV_8uBwYt_DsAS-jw3xQ_I1F0j_-e.title: 09.22 - Obstacles to Bearing Fruits
1keCkV_8uBwYt_DsAS-jw3xQ_I1F0j_-e.summary: BJosh teaches that three specific obstacles choke the word of God and make Christians unfruitful: the cares of this world (worries about school, jobs, relationships, money), the deceitfulness of riches (the false promise that wealth will satisfy when it never does and always flies away), and the lust for other things (uncontrollable desires of the flesh and eyes). He emphasizes that overcoming these requires casting our cares on God who already knows our needs, cultivating godliness with contentment (being satisfied with food and clothing), and seeking first God's kingdom instead of pursuing the same material goals as unbelievers. The solution is trusting that if God gave us His Son, He will freely give us all we need, and recognizing that true gain is godliness combined with contentment, not accumulation of wealth or possessions.

1Zkkp6tRFlP41idolCt2DcVaTDKp66VOs.title: 04.14 - 15 Essential Features of a Sh
1Zkkp6tRFlP41idolCt2DcVaTDKp66VOs.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that Jesus identified Himself as the 'good shepherd' which means there are bad shepherds, and all believers are called to shepherd others with both a wooden rod (gentleness) and an iron rod (ruling/discipline). He emphasizes that the church needs strong pastoral leadership that can rule with authority, set clear boundaries, confront sin directly, and guide people firmly rather than just being gentle and permissive. He stresses that many Christians are called to do the work of ministry according to Ephesians 4:12, not just attend church passively, and that effective shepherding requires strength to tell people hard truths about their sin and direction in life.

1ExNjxZXEw-g3WO9Ye-fuaDZW4sfJ4giY.title: 02.10 - Why This Teaching About Curse
1ExNjxZXEw-g3WO9Ye-fuaDZW4sfJ4giY.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that curses are real spiritual forces released through spoken words that give demons legal access to operate in people's lives, and Christians must have proper respect for curses just as they respect demons. He explains that curses can be national (like on Ghana and Israel), familial (like the curse of Ham preventing building), or personal (from broken promises, parental words, or robbing God in tithes), and these curses cause the world to be turned upside down where those who should prosper struggle instead. He emphasizes that every curse can be neutralized through both the power and wisdom of God working together, and believers should not be offended when pastors point out curses operating in their lives.

1U3K84YqMwlcXvGg8fBUEuT2W2ecLbZeH.title: 04.07 - 15 Essential Features of a Sh
1U3K84YqMwlcXvGg8fBUEuT2W2ecLbZeH.summary: BJosh teaches that essential features of a shepherd include regular fasting, availability for God's work, and faithful tithing to demonstrate true commitment. He emphasizes that faithfulness with money is a requirement for spiritual leadership, stating that those who don't tithe are not真正 members of the church and cannot be trusted with greater spiritual responsibility. He challenges believers to commit the gospel they've heard to faithful people who will teach others, making shepherding and soul-winning a non-negotiable responsibility for every Christian.

1b0A7Yc9NaBDuiuzjxPXtChM9lMyRpTzL.title: 05.05 - The New Creation Pt.2
1b0A7Yc9NaBDuiuzjxPXtChM9lMyRpTzL.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that the unsaved human heart is dead, desperately wicked, deceitful, and darkened, leading to unnatural affections including homosexuality and sexual immorality when God gives people over to their sinful desires. He emphasizes that true salvation through being born again must result in the death of the old man and a complete transformation of nature, not just religious activity while continuing in sin. Using Romans 1:18-32, he argues that when the old nature hasn't died and passed away, it proves a person is not truly saved, regardless of church attendance or religious profession.

1ee1LMJFIuXKOP0PV_egJS4NESzMF4ZrI.title: 05.26 - The New Creation Pt.5
1ee1LMJFIuXKOP0PV_egJS4NESzMF4ZrI.summary: BJosh teaches that when you are born again, your spirit changes but your mind remains the same and must be renewed through God's Word. He emphasizes that true transformation (metamorphosis) comes not from changing your flesh but from renewing your mind through Scripture, with the Holy Spirit removing the veil so you can understand God's Word. He identifies three strongholds Christians must battle: imaginations about how life should be, high things that exalt themselves against knowing God, and thoughts that disobey Christ—all of which can only be defeated by the sword of the Spirit, which is the Word of God.

1GrwRO2kb-2NSV2dnSVA_LYQ3RDXjUr9h.title: 05.19 - The New Creation Pt.4
1GrwRO2kb-2NSV2dnSVA_LYQ3RDXjUr9h.summary: BJosh teaches that when believers are born again, they begin as spiritual babies who must grow through stages—baby, child, and adult—by consistently reading the Word of God and praying. Baby Christians are characterized by carnality, requiring milk (basic teaching), while mature Christians can handle strong meat (deeper doctrine) and become teachers themselves. Growth happens only when believers deliberately desire and consume Scripture, moving from being tossed by every doctrine to becoming rooted, obedient disciples who discern good from evil.

1-Tj2HhnYPLaONXyhaTl82t0Fcz9DW2z4.title: 07.12 - Ho NTC - Where The Fire Goes
1-Tj2HhnYPLaONXyhaTl82t0Fcz9DW2z4.summary: BJosh teaches that spiritual fire in Christians and churches goes out when three essential 'woods' are removed: speaking in tongues, preaching the gospel/soul winning, and signs and wonders. He warns that churches become respectable, comfortable, and dead when they lose the fire of early Pentecost, infected by the leaven of the Pharisees which is hypocrisy and religiosity. He calls believers back to all-night prayer, door-to-door evangelism, crusades, and demonstrating God's power rather than becoming comfortable, professionalized church attenders.

135FHw7Tnii4-os0yahy01NxQZPbtHVFY.title: 06.20 - Dansoma - Understanding The H
135FHw7Tnii4-os0yahy01NxQZPbtHVFY.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that God has already given believers all good gifts through Christ, but these remain hidden and unusable until the Holy Spirit reveals them. He emphasizes that receiving the anointing of the Holy Spirit is not instant or easy—it requires deep knowledge of the Spirit, strong desire, and submission to being led, which costs something significant. The key to unlocking everything God has promised is not human effort or pastoral intervention, but the Holy Spirit revealing what has already been provided but cannot be seen, heard, or felt by natural senses.

1Kn-OfclFFChVvu_J3uKmQwc1KxQOAOCz.title: 08.22 - FLM - Others and Nabal
1Kn-OfclFFChVvu_J3uKmQwc1KxQOAOCz.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that Christians must stop focusing exclusively on their own problems, salvation, and prosperity and instead think about 'others'—the lost souls in communities like Tema who have no church and no access to the gospel. He uses the story of Nabal (a prosperous but selfish man) and David (in the wilderness with nothing) to illustrate that even when we are blessed or still struggling, there are always others in greater need whom God calls us to serve. He challenges the congregation to overcome their selfishness, recognize how much God has already done for them, and actively reach out to start churches and evangelize in unreached areas before it's too late.

1T6JesTyZcnL8f1XJpWUnJ40NFg4FThPb.title: 07.04 - Kasoa - The Holy Spirit
topics: not by might nor by power
1T6JesTyZcnL8f1XJpWUnJ40NFg4FThPb.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that believers are the temple of God and that lasting transformation comes not through miracles, power, or education, but only through the Holy Spirit. He emphasizes that the disciples witnessed Jesus' mighty works yet were not changed until they received the Holy Spirit at Pentecost, demonstrating that the gap between desire and ability to live righteously is bridged only by the Spirit. He stresses the importance of 'waiting on the Lord' to be spiritually charged—spending extended time alone with God in fellowship—to increase spiritual altitude, speed, and power for effective Christian living.

1PlF_czL7KIL9KMl-hhScQQIzcb1uNoHK.title: 05.14 - UPSA - Accessing the Holy Spi
1PlF_czL7KIL9KMl-hhScQQIzcb1uNoHK.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that we are in a spiritual season of the Holy Spirit where physical manifestations are the primary evidence of God's presence, just as the disciples experienced visible fire, audible sound, and shaking in the upper room. He emphasizes that Christians must wait and be planted in the right spiritual season to experience tangible encounters like crying, falling, shaking, laughing, and supernatural phenomena rather than merely receiving intellectual teaching or reminders. He argues that the true Holy Spirit produces physical, measurable evidence of His arrival and presence, citing historical examples from revivals and modern ministers who experienced supernatural manifestations, visions, healings, and miracles.

1Y6i85cLE4Tyc0PkHr5evlHYiSIV0uJ5p.title: 05.29 - Korle Bu - The Holy Spirit
1Y6i85cLE4Tyc0PkHr5evlHYiSIV0uJ5p.summary: Bishop Heward-Mills teaches that Christians must move beyond experiencing the Holy Spirit as merely a force or gift and must meet Him as a Person who transforms their lives. He explains that miracles and power cannot change a person—only a personal encounter with the Holy Spirit Himself brings true transformation, just as the 120 disciples were changed at Pentecost when the Spirit came upon each of them individually. He emphasizes that the Holy Spirit walks beside believers after salvation, waiting to be acknowledged and invited to teach, guide, and make Jesus real in their daily lives.

1rRmoU5n2BZmNW4nnIirOrJKO703FUlMJ.title: 05.31 - Legon - The Holy Spirit
1rRmoU5n2BZmNW4nnIirOrJKO703FUlMJ.summary: BJosh teaches that Jesus had to go away so that the Comforter (the Holy Spirit) could come, emphasizing that the Spirit's coming was dependent on Jesus' departure. He explains that Jesus' physical absence was necessary for the Holy Spirit's arrival and presence with believers. The sermon focuses on understanding the relationship between Jesus' ascension and the Holy Spirit's coming as the Comforter.

1SbfBgMn2wQs1p_KYzlU4jZlkm_vYXLt8.title: 11.06 - ABMTC - Jesus Christ Son Of M
1SbfBgMn2wQs1p_KYzlU4jZlkm_vYXLt8.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that Jesus called Himself the Son of Man to demonstrate the only legal entry point into ministry is through humility, following Jesus' example of becoming a servant despite being God. He emphasizes that pastors must humble themselves at entry points, learn directly from Jesus and His Word rather than their own ideas, and cannot escape the tests and temptations that qualify them for promotion. BJosh challenges ministers that without passing tests of the flesh, submission to authority, and Christ-like humility, they will never receive the anointing or advancement God intends for them.

1Z8k4Kvljmj918pWVSGnv-5hRCzPlSZ5V.title: 06.18 - Wisconsin- 7 Steps to the Ano
topics: not by power nor by might
1Z8k4Kvljmj918pWVSGnv-5hRCzPlSZ5V.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that true spiritual power comes not from human ability, education, or any ritual symbol (like Samson's hair), but solely from the presence of the Holy Spirit—70% of all success is the Holy Spirit, only 30% is human effort. He emphasizes that even Jesus and the apostles waited to receive the Holy Spirit before beginning ministry, and that being around anointed people or having spiritual knowledge is not enough without a personal baptism of the Holy Spirit. Believers must consciously rely on and trust the Holy Spirit in every area of life—marriage, work, ministry, and daily decisions—rather than depending on their own strength, wisdom, or favor.

1vB4JLfBhSj3zas2XuU5NufVn5hJBPthA.title: 10.25 - Oyibi - Jesus And The Man At
1vB4JLfBhSj3zas2XuU5NufVn5hJBPthA.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that Jesus came to break cycles and processes by going directly to the paralyzed man at Bethesda's pool who had waited 38 years, commanding him to rise, take his bed, and walk. He emphasizes that Jesus breaks through human limitations and religious rules to heal immediately, not based on our deserving or ability to follow steps, but by His power and grace alone. The sermon concludes that while physical healing matters, avoiding sin is even more critical because there is something worse than 38 years of paralysis—eternity in hell without Jesus.

1s9McUhckjx3BtYdVnwXaLftCyuEHJ85S.title: 07.11 - Maritime - Quench Not The Hol
1s9McUhckjx3BtYdVnwXaLftCyuEHJ85S.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that believers quench the Holy Spirit when they ignore or dismiss His manifestations through spiritual gifts like the word of wisdom, word of knowledge, and faith. He explains that the Holy Spirit is constantly present in believers' lives trying to guide them, but many Christians don't recognize His voice or respond to His instructions, thereby stopping the flow of God's blessings. He emphasizes that every believer receives manifestations of the Spirit, and quenching occurs when we fail to act on divine wisdom, knowledge, or conviction that the Holy Spirit provides in daily situations.

12aWKIVpeRwF5LxMJjymi1lqBfFsP7pcn.title: 10.10 - Korle Bu - Jesus And The Rich
12aWKIVpeRwF5LxMJjymi1lqBfFsP7pcn.summary: BJosh teaches that Jesus warns against covetousness and spending one's entire life planning for earthly prosperity, comfort, and security rather than focusing on eternal heavenly treasures. Using the parable of the rich fool who built bigger barns only to die that night, he emphasizes that Christians must direct all their wealth, talents, and energy toward God's kingdom instead of accumulating possessions on earth. He challenges believers to lay up treasure in heaven through suffering, tribulation, and faithfulness to the gospel, because earthly achievements and possessions are temporary and will ultimately disappoint.

1XHkpOcasVqjNHuJgpWtgROxOYhZr-o1g.title: 06.12 - ABMTC - The Holy Spirit
1XHkpOcasVqjNHuJgpWtgROxOYhZr-o1g.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that the Holy Spirit's primary manifestation is as teacher and reminder, giving pastors doctrine, reproof, correction, and instruction directly from Scripture through inspiration during quiet time and Bible reading. He emphasizes that without the Holy Spirit actively teaching from the Word, pastors remain helpless, unchanged, and directionless—unable to receive convictions of sin, instructions for ministry decisions, or corrections for their weaknesses. He insists that Scripture is only profitable when given by inspiration of the Holy Spirit, the true author of the Bible, who must open the eyes of Bible students and pastors to see fresh revelation, receive specific guidance, and be transformed into effective ministers.

17N6EKkA9YyWeL7i42R1PytywbYJP4cge.title: 12.15 - Wise as Serpents
17N6EKkA9YyWeL7i42R1PytywbYJP4cge.summary: Bishop Joshua teaches that Christians must learn wisdom from serpents who have mastered overcoming their handicaps of having no hands, legs, ears, or functional eyes, yet thrive everywhere as successful predators. He emphasizes that God does not remove our problems and afflictions, but gives us the power to overcome and turn our weaknesses into strengths, just as serpents use their limitations to their advantage. Using personal stories and examples, he demonstrates that what appears to be a handicap—whether youth, disability, or other challenges—can actually become one's greatest strength when approached with godly wisdom.

1S2EdtLkrCXX9za2GJcMAtm2We53DeaTO.title: 06.09 - Why Non-Tithing Christians ar
1S2EdtLkrCXX9za2GJcMAtm2We53DeaTO.summary: BJosh teaches that non-tithing Christians remain poor because they have no seed to harvest, they do not attract God's blessings through obedience, and they live under the curse on the ground without accessing heaven's open windows.
